deep_dive.title: Self-Paced Curriculum Reinforcement Learning for Autonomous Superbike Racing in Simulation
deep_dive.abstract: Autonomous Racing has seen remarkable progress through deep Reinforcement Learning (RL), primarily for four-wheeled vehicles. However, motorbikes introduce substantially greater complexity due to the need to manage balance and lean angle, in addition to more reactive steering and throttle control, and a smaller weight. In this work, we present a framework for training an autonomous agent to race a superbike in VRider SBK, a physics-accurate Unity-based motorbike simulator. Our approach integrates Soft Actor-Critic (SAC) with Self-Paced curriculum Deep reinforcement Learning (SPDL), which dynamically generates progressively more challenging tasks based on the agent's performance, without requiring manual curriculum design. The agent's state space comprises proprioceptive features extended with lean-angle history, along with global track features via course points. The reward signal is shaped to encourage progress along the track while penalizing instability-inducing behaviors specific to two-wheeled dynamics. Preliminary experimental results demonstrate that SPDL outperforms SAC alone in training efficiency, lap time, and driving stability across multiple tracks and motorbike models, establishing a first baseline for RL-based autonomous motorbike racing.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: AI agent trained to race a superbike in a physics-accurate simulator
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 在物理精确的摩托车模拟器中训练AI智能体进行比赛
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 在物理精确的摩托车模拟器中训练AI智能体进行比赛
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 在物理精确的摩托车模拟器中训练AI智能体进行比赛
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper presents a framework using Self-Paced Curriculum RL to train an AI agent to autonomously race a superbike in a realistic simulation, directly addressing AI agent playing games.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有自动驾驶赛车研究多针对四轮车辆，二轮摩托车因需平衡与倾斜控制而更复杂。本文提出基于软演员-评论家与自定课程深度强化学习的框架，在VRider SBK模拟器中训练智能体进行超级摩托车比赛。智能体无需人工设计课程即可动态应对难度递增任务。实验表明该方法能有效学习稳定操控与高速竞速策略。该工作为游戏环境中的AI智能体训练提供了直接范例。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有自动驾驶赛车研究多针对四轮车辆，二轮摩托车因需平衡与倾斜控制而更复杂。本文提出基于软演员-评论家与自定课程深度强化学习的框架，在VRider SBK模拟器中训练智能体进行超级摩托车比赛。智能体无需人工设计课程即可动态应对难度递增任务。实验表明该方法能有效学习稳定操控与高速竞速策略。该工作为游戏环境中的AI智能体训练提供了直接范例。

deep_dive.title: Phi-Actor-Critic: Steering General-Sum Games to Pareto-Efficient Correlated Equilibria
deep_dive.abstract: Real-world multi-agent systems, from traffic coordination to resource allocation, are often modeled as general-sum games where individual incentives conflict with collective welfare. In these settings, the central challenge is not merely finding an equilibrium, but selecting socially desirable outcomes among many suboptimal Nash equilibria. Standard deep multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) methods struggle with this problem, as value-decomposition approaches are constrained by monotonicity assumptions and policy-gradient methods often converge to stable but socially inefficient equilibria. To address this limitation, we propose $Φ$-Actor-Critic ($Φ$-AC), a framework that leverages swap regret minimization to steer learning toward high-welfare correlated equilibria (CE). To make counterfactual regret estimation tractable in deep MARL, $Φ$-AC employs a centralized attention critic that predicts vector-valued regrets in a single forward pass, avoiding computationally expensive counterfactual simulations. We further introduce a Lagrangian-based equilibrium selection mechanism that optimizes social welfare while enforcing stability through regret constraints. Experiments on matrix games, Multi-Agent Particle Environments (MPE), and the Melting Pot Harvest scenario demonstrate that $Φ$-AC learns efficient and stable coordination strategies across diverse mixed-motive settings while maintaining high collective return and competitive fairness.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: MARL method for general-sum games steering to Pareto-efficient equilibria
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于一般和博弈的MARL方法，引导至帕累托有效均衡
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于一般和博弈的MARL方法，引导至帕累托有效均衡
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于一般和博弈的MARL方法，引导至帕累托有效均衡
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Phi-Actor-Critic uses swap regret minimization to steer multi-agent learning toward Pareto-efficient correlated equilibria in general-sum games, directly applicable to training AI agents playing games.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出Φ-Actor-Critic（Φ-AC）框架，利用交换遗憾最小化引导多智能体在一般和博弈中达到帕累托有效的关联均衡。该方法克服了标准MARL方法收敛至低效纳什均衡的问题，适用于游戏环境中AI代理的训练与协调，实现更优的集体福利。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文提出Φ-Actor-Critic（Φ-AC）框架，利用交换遗憾最小化引导多智能体在一般和博弈中达到帕累托有效的关联均衡。该方法克服了标准MARL方法收敛至低效纳什均衡的问题，适用于游戏环境中AI代理的训练与协调，实现更优的集体福利。

quick_skim.title: 3SPO: State-Score-Supervised Policy Optimization for LLM Agents
quick_skim.abstract: Training large language models (LLMs) as autonomous agents via reinforcement learning (RL) has enabled frontier models to achieve superhuman performance in long-horizon tasks. However, existing RL algorithms operate at the trajectory level, performing policy optimization only after collecting complete episode rollouts. This coarse-grained approach faces fundamental challenges in multi-turn agent settings where rewards are sparse, delayed, and credit assignment across individual steps is critical. In this work, we propose \textbf{State-Score-Supervised Policy Optimization (3SPO)}, a novel RL algorithm that performs post-step policy optimization with dynamic state score supervision. At each step, 3SPO computes the state score based on historical success rates, supervising step-wise credit assignment, adaptive rollout and post-step policy optimization without requiring value function estimation or additional auxiliary models. Theoretically, under a per-state bandit abstraction, we show that the proposed score-supervised allocation mechanism achieves logarithmic allocation regret and provide sample-complexity guarantees for action identification, score distinguishability, and filtering stability. Experiments on ALFWorld and WebShop with Qwen2.5-1.5B/7B-Instruct show that 3SPO consistently outperforms GRPO by $+22.6\%$ on ALFWorld and $+15.6$ points on WebShop, while using comparable resources to achieve $2.4\times$ more state exploration and $1.8\times$ faster convergence. Code is available at https://github.com/genalyu/3SPO.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: proposes step-level RL algorithm that can be applied to game agent training
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出可在游戏智能体训练中应用的按步强化学习算法
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出可在游戏智能体训练中应用的按步强化学习算法
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出可在游戏智能体训练中应用的按步强化学习算法
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: 3SPO introduces step-level policy optimization with state scores for LLM agents, a method transferable to game environments with sparse rewards.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有强化学习算法在稀疏奖励的多回合智能体任务中信用分配困难。本文提出状态分数监督策略优化（3SPO），在每一步基于历史成功率计算状态分数，实现按步策略更新。在长时程LLM智能体任务中，3SPO显著优于轨迹级方法。该算法可迁移至游戏环境中AI智能体的训练，提升稀疏奖励场景下的学习效率。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有强化学习算法在稀疏奖励的多回合智能体任务中信用分配困难。本文提出状态分数监督策略优化（3SPO），在每一步基于历史成功率计算状态分数，实现按步策略更新。在长时程LLM智能体任务中，3SPO显著优于轨迹级方法。该算法可迁移至游戏环境中AI智能体的训练，提升稀疏奖励场景下的学习效率。
